---
title: "nitrix.smoothing.metric"
generated: true
signature-hash: a5c90736ba8d9ff8
description: "Feature-space metrics for the bounded bilateral filter."
listing:
  id: nitrix-smoothing-metric-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-smoothing-metric/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.smoothing.metric`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Feature-space metrics for the bounded bilateral filter.

[`bilateral_gaussian`](nitrix-smoothing-bilateral/bilateral_gaussian.qmd) weights neighbours by a Gaussian over a metric
on feature space,

$$
w_{ij} = \exp\left(
    -\tfrac{1}{2}\,(f_i - f_j)^{\top} M (f_i - f_j)
\right)
$$

where $M \succeq 0$ is supplied **factored** as $M = L L^{\top}$
with $L$ of shape $(d_f, k)$, $k \le d_f$.  The kernel
never needs $M$ itself: every weight depends on the feature
difference only through the projection $z = L^{\top} (f_i - f_j)$ and
the squared norm $q = z^{\top} z = (f_i - f_j)^{\top} M (f_i - f_j)$.
This module supplies that projection as a small algebraic data type.

Three tiers, all feeding the identical kernel (only the shape and source
of the factor changes):

- [`DiagonalMetric`](nitrix-smoothing-metric/DiagonalMetric.qmd) -- one bandwidth per feature; $M$ is
  $\operatorname{diag}(1 / \sigma^2)$.  The cheap, interpretable
  default; recovers the classic per-channel bilateral.
- [`FactorMetric`](nitrix-smoothing-metric/FactorMetric.qmd) -- a general factor $L$ of shape
  $(d_f, k)$.  $k < d_f$ gives a **low-rank** metric that
  projects correlated feature channels into a $k$-dimensional
  discriminative subspace before the norm (cost $O(k)$ per neighbour
  rather than $O(d_f)$); $k = d_f$ gives a general anisotropic
  metric.

Constructors compose the tiers without leaving the substrate:
[`block_diagonal_metric`](nitrix-smoothing-metric/block_diagonal_metric.qmd) assembles a block-diagonal $L$
(independent per-modality bandwidths -- e.g. a tissue-intensity block
weighted separately from a functional-correlation block), and
[`metric_from_spd`](nitrix-smoothing-metric/metric_from_spd.qmd) factors an explicit SPD $M$ via Cholesky.

Both metric types are registered JAX pytrees whose array field is the
single leaf, so a metric flows through `jit` / `vmap` and is
differentiable end-to-end: the factor $L$ is a learnable parameter
if the consumer wants one.  Data-driven *fitting* of $L$ (population
PCA, supervised metric learning) is deliberately out of scope -- that is a
modelling concern for a consumer, built from [`nitrix.stats`](nitrix-stats.qmd) covariance
tools and [`nitrix.linalg`](nitrix-linalg.qmd); this module supplies only the pure
mechanism.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `d_f` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-smoothing-metric-symbols}
:::
